# UC-22.2.11 ¬∑ NIS2 Cryptography and Encryption Policy Monitoring

## Description

Surfaces X.509 certificates whose `ssl_end_time` falls inside a thirty-day horizon using accelerated CIM Certificate summaries so crypto governance teams can renew before services break or downgrade ciphers.

## Value

Expired orsoon-to-expire keys silently undermine encrypted channels that regulators expect to stay trustworthy; anchoring renewals to Splunk schedules gives measurable lead time, owner accountability, and historical proof that the organization actively managed cryptography‚ÄĒnot just bought it once.

## Implementation

(1) Install CIM ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) and accelerate **Certificates**. (2) Forward Stream `stream:tls` to `index=pki`. (3) Import inventory scanner output as `certificate:inventory`. (4) Map Tenable plugin families about weak crypto to `index=vulnerability`. (5) Maintain `nis2_crypto_exception_register.csv` with compensating controls. (6) Email PKI ops when `status=CRITICAL`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Splunk Cloud Victoria experience or on-prem indexers sized for TLS mirror traffic (~5‚Äď15 MB/min per 10 Gbps span port after filtering).
‚ÄĘ Splunk Add-on for Stream ([Splunkbase 1809](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809)) deployed on Stream Forwarders observing east-west VLANs hosting APIs; `inputs.conf` enables TLS metadata-only (no payload capture) to reduce GDPR risk.
‚ÄĘ Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) ‚Č•5.3 with Certificates model acceleration enabled for at least 90 days of retention.
‚ÄĘ Optional Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) for asset lookups tying `ssl_subject_common_name` to business owner via `assets_by_str`.
‚ÄĘ PKI team roster in `nis2_pki_owner.csv` keyed by `ssl_issuer_common_name`.

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
‚ÄĘ Stream: configure **Configure > Protocols > TLS** capturing `tls_version`, `cipher`, `server_name`, `cert_expiry`. Set `index=pki` `sourcetype=stream:tls`.
‚ÄĘ Certificate inventory: nightly scanner writes JSON to HEC with `sourcetype=certificate:inventory` fields `notBefore`, `notAfter`, `subject`, `issuer`.
‚ÄĘ props.conf example for inventory:
```
[certificate:inventory]
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ
TRUNCATE = 10000
```
‚ÄĘ Validate ingestion rate: `| tstats count where index=pki OR index=certificates by sourcetype span=1h` ‚ÄĒ flatlines mean forwarder failure.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Use the `spl` field for CIM-first renewal queue. Supplement with Stream weak-protocol search saved as `UC-22.2.11-stream`:
```spl
index=pki sourcetype=stream:tls earliest=-24h tls_version=*
| eval weak=if(match(tls_version,"SSLv|TLSv1\\.0|TLSv1\\.1"),1,0)
| where weak=1
| stats count by dest_ip tls_version
```
Chain both in a **Search Processing Language ‚Üí union** panel or use correlation search in ES.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
‚ÄĘ Choose a known service (e.g., `sap-api.internal`): compare Splunk `ssl_end_time` to `openssl s_client -connect` output; skew >24h implies TZ mis-parse.
‚ÄĘ For Tenable findings, compare plugin `57582` output for the same CN.
‚ÄĘ Confirm acceleration: `| datamodel Certificates All_Certificates search` returns rows in <5s.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
‚ÄĘ Dashboard tabs: **Renewals**, **Weak TLS**, **Exception register**.
‚ÄĘ Runbook: assign `ssl_subject` to PKI queue in ServiceNow `sn_sec_cmn` using alert action.
‚ÄĘ Monthly PDF signed with `evidenceSigning` stored under `index=audit_evidence`.

Step 5 ‚ÄĒ Troubleshooting
‚ÄĘ **tstats zero rows** ‚ÄĒ acceleration off; **Settings ‚Üí Data models ‚Üí Certificates ‚Üí Edit ‚Üí Acceleration**.
‚ÄĘ **Stream sees only TLS 1.0** ‚ÄĒ mirror VLAN mis-configured; verify span port using `tcpdump -nn port 443`.
‚ÄĘ **Duplicate `ssl_subject_common_name`** from multi-region DCs ‚ÄĒ dedup by `min(ssl_end_time)`.
‚ÄĘ **False EXPIRED** ‚ÄĒ host clock skew on forwarder; check NTP.


## SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates
  where All_Certificates.ssl_end_time < relative_time(now(), "+30d")
  by All_Certificates.ssl_subject_common_name All_Certificates.ssl_end_time All_Certificates.ssl_issuer_common_name
| rename All_Certificates.* as *
| eval days_until_expiry=round((ssl_end_time - now())/86400, 0)
| eval status=case(days_until_expiry < 0, "EXPIRED", days_until_expiry < 14, "CRITICAL", days_until_expiry < 30, "WARNING")
| sort days_until_expiry
| table ssl_subject_common_name, ssl_issuer_common_name, days_until_expiry, status
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Certificates.x509_ssl_hash) from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by Certificates.ssl_subject | head 10000
```

## Visualization

Table of expiring subjects, pie chart of issuer share, overlay of renewal tickets from ServiceNow.
